project.name: versus-engine
project.description: A category-agnostic engine that turns database records into animated "X vs Y" comparison videos — cars, phones, laptops, or anything else — without writing a line of category-specific code. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: AudioTrack.tsx
nodes.summary: Remotion component that renders the composition's looping background music and time-positioned SFX cues, driving the music volume with the per-frame ducking curve from createMusicVolumeFn.

nodes.name: music.ts
nodes.summary: Pure audio-math utilities that build the per-frame music volume envelope: base level, dB-to-linear conversion, -6dB ducking around SFX/narration cues, and a 2-second fade-out.
nodes.languageNotes: Returns a closure (frame: number) => number rather than a value, so the ducking curve can be evaluated lazily per rendered frame.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Shared audio types for the studio renderer: the SfxCue interface and the sfxSrc helper that resolves a cue name to its static asset path.

nodes.name: Background.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the animated scene backdrop: a theme-colored gradient plus one of three motif overlays (speed lines, circuit pattern, or drifting grid) selected by theme.motif.

nodes.name: Confetti.tsx
nodes.summary: Physics-based confetti particle burst used in the winner-reveal celebration, computing per-particle trajectories with gravity and randomized seeds via Remotion's deterministic random().
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Remotion's seeded random() (not Math.random()) so particle trajectories render identically on every frame pass, which is required for deterministic video rendering.

nodes.name: Comparison16x9.tsx
nodes.summary: The root 16:9 Remotion composition: validates VideoInput, resolves theme/layout/durations, sequences all six scenes with TransitionSeries (fade/slide), overlays the persistent Scoreboard, and mounts AudioTrack. This is the entry point Phase 1 renders end-to-end per CLAUDE.md.
nodes.languageNotes: Scene modules are imported with `import * as` and stored in a SEQUENTIAL_SCENES array so getDuration/getSfxCues/the component itself can be invoked generically across scenes without a switch statement.

nodes.name: layout.ts
nodes.summary: Derives the SceneLayout (landscape vs portrait orientation) once per composition from VideoInput.meta.aspect, so scenes never re-derive it themselves.

nodes.name: AudioTrack
nodes.summary: React component rendering the looping <Audio> music bed with a computed ducking volume function, plus one <Sequence> per SFX cue.

nodes.name: dbToLinear
nodes.summary: Converts a decibel value to a linear gain multiplier (10^(db/20)) used throughout the volume envelope math.

nodes.name: createMusicVolumeFn
nodes.summary: Builds a per-frame volume function for the looping music bed: ducks -6dB around each SFX/narration cue with attack/hold/release timing, and fades out over the final two seconds of the composition.

nodes.name: sfxSrc
nodes.summary: Resolves a bare SFX cue name (e.g. "whoosh") to its static asset path under assets/sfx/.

nodes.name: SpeedLines
nodes.summary: Internal motif renderer drawing 14 diagonal streak lines that drift across the frame; used when theme.motif is "speed-lines".

nodes.name: Circuit
nodes.summary: Internal motif renderer drawing a repeating SVG circuit-board pattern; used when theme.motif is "circuit".

nodes.name: Grid
nodes.summary: Internal motif renderer drawing a slowly drifting grid with a radial mask fade; used when theme.motif is "grid".

nodes.name: Background
nodes.summary: Renders the theme-colored gradient backdrop and selects one of SpeedLines/Circuit/Grid based on theme.motif; used behind every scene.

nodes.name: Confetti
nodes.summary: Simulates 60 confetti particles with randomized angle/speed/color/rotation and gravity-based trajectories, culling particles once they fall offscreen.

nodes.name: computeTotalDurationInFrames
nodes.summary: Sums each sequential scene's duration and subtracts overlapping transition frames to get the composition's total frame count.

nodes.name: Comparison16x9
nodes.summary: Main composition component: validates props, resolves theme/layout/durations, wires Intro/ContenderReveal/SpecBattle/WinnerReveal/Outro into a TransitionSeries with fade/slide transitions, overlays Scoreboard, and mounts AudioTrack.

nodes.name: getLayout
nodes.summary: Maps VideoInput.meta.aspect ("9:16" vs otherwise) to a SceneLayout orientation of "portrait" or "landscape".

nodes.name: ContenderReveal.tsx
nodes.summary: Scene 2 of the comparison video: spring-animated contender cards (image, name, brand, price pill) that slide in from alternating sides in landscape or stack vertically in portrait.

nodes.name: Intro.tsx
nodes.summary: Opening title scene showing the "X VS Y" matchup title split from the video's title string, with interpolated logo fade-out and title scale/opacity entrances plus a LogoSting overlay.

nodes.name: Outro.tsx
nodes.summary: Closing scene with a subscribe call-to-action, a spring-animated bell icon, and a category-specific teaser line for the next matchup.

nodes.name: Scoreboard.tsx
nodes.summary: Persistent corner overlay tracking each contender's round-win tally during the spec-battle and winner-reveal window, popping the score with a spring animation whenever a round concludes.

nodes.name: WinnerReveal.tsx
nodes.summary: Final verdict scene: reveals the winning contender with a Crown, a Confetti burst colored by contender accents, the score line, and the verdict tagline.

nodes.name: schema.ts
nodes.summary: Re-exports the Zod-validated VideoInput contract from @versus-engine/shared so the Remotion renderer and the comparison builder validate against the exact same schema, honoring the project's "video JSON contract is sacred" rule.
nodes.languageNotes: Single `export * from "@versus-engine/shared"` statement — the actual Zod schema and VideoInput type live in the shared workspace package, not in apps/studio.

nodes.name: timing.ts
nodes.summary: Re-exports scene-duration constants (TIMING_SECONDS) and the secondsToFrames/contenderRevealSeconds/specBattleSeconds helpers from @versus-engine/shared so the renderer and the analytics feedback loop share identical timing math.
nodes.languageNotes: Named re-export from @versus-engine/shared, not local definitions — keeps timing numbers usable outside the Remotion-only package (e.g. by apps/workers for retention analytics).

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Returns this scene's duration in frames, scaled by contender count via contenderRevealSeconds.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Returns the scene's single "whoosh" SFX cue fired at frame 0.

nodes.name: ContenderCard
nodes.summary: Internal card component rendering one contender's image, name, brand, and price pill, spring-animated to slide in from alternating sides (or stack in portrait).

nodes.name: ContenderReveal
nodes.summary: Scene component laying out one ContenderCard per contender over the animated Background, adapting flex direction for portrait vs landscape.

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Returns the intro scene's fixed duration in frames from TIMING_SECONDS.intro.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Returns the intro's single "whoosh" SFX cue at frame 0.

nodes.name: Intro
nodes.summary: Opening scene that splits the video title on "vs" to show each contender name, animates logo fade-out and title scale/opacity, and renders the LogoSting over the Background.

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Returns the outro scene's fixed duration in frames from TIMING_SECONDS.outro.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Returns the outro's single "whoosh" SFX cue at frame 0.

nodes.name: Outro
nodes.summary: Closing scene with a spring-animated bell icon, subscribe call-to-action text, and a category-specific teaser line for the next video.

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Returns the scoreboard overlay's duration, spanning the spec-battle rounds plus the winner-reveal window.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Returns an empty SFX cue list; the scoreboard overlay has no dedicated sound effects of its own.

nodes.name: Scoreboard
nodes.summary: Persistent corner overlay computing each contender's round-win tally from elapsed frames and popping the score with a spring animation on each new round.

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Returns the winner-reveal scene's fixed duration in frames from TIMING_SECONDS.winnerReveal.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Returns the verdict's SFX cue, defaulting to "drumroll-confetti", fired at frame 0.

nodes.name: WinnerReveal
nodes.summary: Final scene revealing the winning contender with a Crown, a Confetti burst colored by contender accents, the score line, and the verdict tagline.

nodes.name: analytics-sync.ts
nodes.summary: `pnpm analytics:sync` CLI that pulls per-video stats and audience retention from the YouTube Analytics API v2 for every published Upload, persists them to VideoAnalytics/RoundRetention, and correlates the retention curve back to individual rounds using the exact scene timing the studio rendered with. Idempotent — each run fully replaces that upload's stored analytics.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Promise.all to fetch headline stats, impression stats, and the retention curve concurrently per upload, then a Prisma $transaction to atomically replace prior RoundRetention rows.

nodes.name: lambda-deploy-function.ts
nodes.summary: `pnpm lambda:deploy-function` one-time/upgrade CLI that deploys the Remotion Lambda render function itself and prints the resulting REMOTION_LAMBDA_FUNCTION_NAME/REMOTION_AWS_REGION values to add to .env.

nodes.name: lambda-deploy-site.ts
nodes.summary: `pnpm lambda:deploy-site` CLI that bundles apps/studio and uploads it to the Remotion Lambda S3 bucket, printing the resulting REMOTION_LAMBDA_SERVE_URL to add to .env; must be re-run after every studio change that should ship to Lambda renders.

nodes.name: worker.ts
nodes.summary: `pnpm worker` long-running process entry point that starts the render and publish BullMQ consumers and wires SIGINT/SIGTERM to a graceful shutdown that closes both workers before exiting.

nodes.name: lambda-config.ts
nodes.summary: Shared configuration helpers for Remotion Lambda: detects whether RENDER_TARGET=lambda, validates/reads the AWS region, and assembles the region/functionName/serveUrl config needed to invoke an already-deployed Lambda function and site.

nodes.name: publish-queue-client.ts
nodes.summary: Producer-side BullMQ Queue client (lazily initialized, memoized connection) used by the render worker to enqueue a publish job once a render completes.

nodes.name: retention-correlation.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for retention-correlation.ts, covering linear interpolation between retention curve points, clamping behavior at the curve's ends, empty-curve handling, and per-round retention/drop-off mapping including the null specKey fallback.

nodes.name: retention-correlation.ts
nodes.summary: Pure math module that turns a YouTube audienceRetention curve into a per-round retention snapshot (retention at start/end and drop-off), reusing the same round timing the studio used to render the video.
nodes.languageNotes: retentionAtRatio implements manual linear interpolation over a sorted curve with clamping at both ends — a small but easy-to-get-wrong numerical routine, which is why it has dedicated unit tests.

nodes.name: youtube-analytics-client.ts
nodes.summary: Builds an authenticated YouTube Analytics API v2 client, reusing the same OAuth app and refresh token as the Data API v3 publish client.

nodes.name: youtube-client.ts
nodes.summary: OAuth2 client construction and env-var validation shared by the YouTube auth CLI and the publish worker; exposes an authenticated YouTube Data API v3 client built from a refresh token.

nodes.name: publish-worker.ts
nodes.summary: BullMQ consumer for the publish queue that uploads a Comparison's completed render to YouTube via the Data API v3, honoring Upload.scheduledAt for future-dated releases and setting an A/B thumbnail variant; safely retried since it no-ops if the Upload already has a videoId.
nodes.languageNotes: readableFor() abstracts over local disk paths vs. S3 HTTP URLs so the same upload code path works for both local and Lambda-rendered outputs.

nodes.name: render-worker.ts
nodes.summary: BullMQ consumer for the render queue that renders a Comparison's frozen videoJson through either a local @remotion/renderer bundle or Remotion Lambda (depending on RENDER_TARGET), produces A/B thumbnail variants, tracks progress in RenderJob, and enqueues the follow-up publish job.
nodes.languageNotes: renderLocally() and renderOnLambda() implement the same RenderResult contract via two very different mechanisms (in-process renderMedia vs. polling getRenderProgress), letting processRenderJob() branch on isLambdaRenderTarget() without leaking Lambda details elsewhere.

nodes.name: fetchHeadlineStats
nodes.summary: Queries the YouTube Analytics API for views, average view duration, and average view percentage for a single video over a date range.

nodes.name: fetchImpressionStats
nodes.summary: Best-effort fetch of impressions and impressions click-through rate for a video, swallowing and warning on errors since these Studio metrics aren't available on every channel.

nodes.name: fetchRetentionCurve
nodes.summary: Fetches the audience-retention curve (elapsedVideoTimeRatio vs. audienceWatchRatio) for a video from the YouTube Analytics API.

nodes.name: syncUpload
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a single upload's analytics sync: loads the frozen videoJson, fetches headline/impression/retention stats concurrently, upserts VideoAnalytics, and replaces RoundRetention rows in a transaction using the studio's own round timing.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry function that loads all published uploads with a videoId, syncs each one's analytics, and reports a success/failure count, exiting non-zero if every sync failed.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Deploys the Remotion Lambda render function in the configured AWS region and prints the env vars to persist.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Gets or creates the Lambda S3 bucket, deploys the bundled studio site to it, and prints the resulting serve URL to persist.

nodes.name: isLambdaRenderTarget
nodes.summary: Returns true when RENDER_TARGET is set to "lambda", used by the render worker to choose between local and Lambda rendering.

nodes.name: getLambdaRegion
nodes.summary: Reads REMOTION_AWS_REGION (defaulting to us-east-1) and validates it against Remotion Lambda's supported region list.

nodes.name: getLambdaRenderConfig
nodes.summary: Assembles the region, function name, and serve URL required to invoke an already-deployed Lambda function/site, throwing if required env vars are missing.

nodes.name: getPublishQueue
nodes.summary: Lazily creates and memoizes the BullMQ Queue + Redis connection used to enqueue publish jobs.

nodes.name: retentionAtRatio
nodes.summary: Linearly interpolates the audience watch ratio at an arbitrary point on the video timeline, clamping at the retention curve's first and last points.

nodes.name: computeRoundRetention
nodes.summary: Maps each round's start/end time range onto the retention curve to produce a per-round retention-at-start, retention-at-end, and drop-off value.

nodes.name: getYoutubeAnalyticsClient
nodes.summary: Builds an authenticated YouTube Analytics API v2 client using the shared OAuth2 client and a refresh token from the environment.

nodes.name: requireEnv
nodes.summary: Reads a required environment variable, throwing a descriptive error pointing to .env.example when it's missing.

nodes.name: getOAuthClient
nodes.summary: Constructs a Google OAuth2 client from the YouTube client id/secret/redirect URI env vars, used both by the auth CLI and by API client builders.

nodes.name: getYoutubeClient
nodes.summary: Builds an authenticated YouTube Data API v3 client using the shared OAuth2 client and a refresh token, used by the publish worker.

nodes.name: readableFor
nodes.summary: Returns a readable stream for a render output regardless of whether it's a local disk path or an S3 HTTP URL, unifying local vs. Lambda render outputs for upload.

nodes.name: processPublishJob
nodes.summary: Publishes a comparison's completed render to YouTube: no-ops if already uploaded, fetches the latest completed RenderJob, inserts the video with scheduled/immediate privacy settings, sets the A/B-selected thumbnail, and marks the Upload done.

nodes.name: startPublishWorker
nodes.summary: Creates and returns the BullMQ Worker for the publish queue, validating job payloads with Zod, marking the Upload FAILED on error, and logging completion/failure events.

nodes.name: getBundleLocation
nodes.summary: Lazily bundles the Remotion studio entry point once (memoized promise), overriding webpack's extension resolution so the shared package's ".js"-suffixed relative imports resolve correctly.

nodes.name: renderLocally
nodes.summary: Renders a comparison's video and both thumbnail A/B variants in-process via @remotion/renderer, writing to output/ and periodically persisting render progress to the RenderJob row.

nodes.name: renderOnLambda
nodes.summary: Kicks off a Remotion Lambda render and thumbnail renders, polling getRenderProgress until done or fatally failed, updating RenderJob progress along the way; requires the Lambda function/site to already be deployed.

nodes.name: processRenderJob
nodes.summary: Marks a RenderJob running, loads the comparison's frozen videoJson, dispatches to local or Lambda rendering based on RENDER_TARGET, records the result, and enqueues the follow-up publish job.

nodes.name: enqueuePendingPublish
nodes.summary: Finds the oldest queued Upload for a comparison with no render yet and enqueues its publish job, delayed until Upload.scheduledAt so YouTube upload timing matches the requested cadence.

nodes.name: startRenderWorker
nodes.summary: Creates and returns the BullMQ Worker for the render queue, validating job payloads with Zod, marking the RenderJob FAILED on error, and logging completion/failure events.

nodes.name: build-comparison.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma-backed orchestrator that loads VERIFIED products and their SpecValues, runs round selection and scoring, generates (or falls back to deterministic) metadata, and freezes the result into the Zod-validated VideoInput contract; also idempotently upserts the built comparison keyed by slug.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses inversion of control for narration synthesis (an injected async function) so this package never depends directly on @versus-engine/narration or a TTS provider.

nodes.name: metadata.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for metadata.ts covering prompt construction, response parsing, and the AI-generation/fallback metadata paths using a mocked Anthropic-like client.

nodes.name: metadata.ts
nodes.summary: Generates AI-written YouTube title/description/tags/tagline for a comparison via an injectable Anthropic-like client, with prompt building, response parsing, and a deterministic fallback used when no client is configured.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors the AnthropicLike-interface pattern from packages/ingestion/src/normalize/ai-normalizer.ts so it stays testable without a live API key.

nodes.name: retention-boost.ts
nodes.summary: Phase 5 analytics feedback loop that converts historical YouTube retention data (RoundRetention rows) into a specKey-to-multiplier boost map consumed by round-selection.ts, favoring specs that retain viewers better than the category average.

nodes.name: round-selection.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for round-selection.ts's scoring, winner-computation, and selectRounds heuristic, including the price-always-included, badge-cap, and per-contender win-guarantee rules.

nodes.name: round-selection.ts
nodes.summary: Implements the CLAUDE.md round-selection heuristic: scores each SpecDefinition candidate by priorityWeight x normalizedDifference (with an optional retention boost), picks the top 6-8, always includes price, caps badge rounds at one, and guarantees each contender wins at least one round when the data allows.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses type-narrowing predicates (numbers.every((n): n is number => ...)) to safely coerce optional numeric/boolean spec values before ranking.

nodes.name: scoring.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit test verifying computeVerdict correctly tallies round winners into per-contender scores and resolves (or ties) the overall winner index.

nodes.name: scoring.ts
nodes.summary: Tiny pure function that tallies an array of per-round winner indexes into an overall verdict (winnerIndex and scores), treating ties as no winner.

nodes.name: video-json-builder.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for buildVideoInput covering round encoding, SFX selection, verdict computation, and schema validation of the assembled VideoInput contract.

nodes.name: video-json-builder.ts
nodes.summary: Pure builder that turns selected RoundCandidates and contender metadata into the Zod-validated VideoInput contract — encoding round values, picking SFX cues, computing the overall verdict, and resolving aspect-ratio resolution defaults.
nodes.languageNotes: Keeps persistence (build-comparison.ts) fully separate from this pure transform, so the VideoInput shape can be unit-tested without a database.

nodes.name: buildComparison
nodes.summary: Loads VERIFIED products and their SpecValues for the given productIds, validates they share a category, builds RoundCandidates, runs round selection and scoring, generates metadata, and assembles the final VideoInput (without touching the database).

nodes.name: saveComparison
nodes.summary: Idempotently upserts a BuiltComparison and its ComparisonContender rows, freezing videoJson exactly as built, keyed by the comparison's slug.

nodes.name: buildAndSaveComparison
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that runs buildComparison followed by saveComparison and returns both results.

nodes.name: buildMetadataPrompt
nodes.summary: Builds the LLM prompt instructing the model to return YouTube title/description/tags/tagline JSON given the category, contenders, round results, and verdict.

nodes.name: parseMetadataResponse
nodes.summary: Strips optional markdown code-fences from an LLM response and JSON-parses the remaining text.

nodes.name: generateComparisonMetadata
nodes.summary: Calls the injected Anthropic-like client with the metadata prompt, extracts the text block, and validates the parsed JSON against the comparisonMetadataSchema.

nodes.name: fallbackComparisonMetadata
nodes.summary: Deterministically synthesizes title, description, tags, and tagline from contenders/rounds/verdict when no Anthropic client is configured, e.g. for local dev without an API key.

nodes.name: getRetentionBoosts
nodes.summary: Aggregates RoundRetention rows by specKey (with a minimum sample threshold), scores each spec relative to the category's average drop-off, and returns a clamped boost multiplier map for round-selection scoring.

nodes.name: numberCandidate
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a numeric RoundCandidate fixture with sensible defaults, overridable per test case.

nodes.name: numericEncoding
nodes.summary: Coerces a RoundCandidate's per-contender values into a numeric array when the dataType is NUMBER or BOOLEAN, or null when any value is missing or the type is unrankable.

nodes.name: normalizedDifference
nodes.summary: Computes the relative spread between contenders for a candidate spec — a normalized numeric range, or a binary 0/1 signal for free-text specs that differ at all.

nodes.name: scoreCandidate
nodes.summary: Scores a RoundCandidate as priorityWeight times normalizedDifference times an optional retention boost multiplier (defaulting to 1).

nodes.name: computeRoundWinner
nodes.summary: Determines which contender index wins a round based on numeric encoding and higherIsBetter, returning null for ties or non-rankable (text) rounds.

nodes.name: selectRounds
nodes.summary: Implements the full round-selection heuristic: scores and ranks candidates, always includes price, takes the top 6-8 while capping badge rounds at one, backfills to the minimum if needed, and guarantees each contender at least one win before restoring input order.

nodes.name: guaranteeEachContenderAWin
nodes.summary: For each contender with zero round wins in the selected set, finds an unselected candidate they'd win and swaps it in for the weakest eligible selected round, respecting the badge cap.

nodes.name: findSwapOutIndex
nodes.summary: Finds the lowest-scoring selected round (excluding price, and excluding any round that is a contender's sole win) that can be safely swapped out during the win-guarantee pass.

nodes.name: computeVerdict
nodes.summary: Tallies an array of per-round winner indexes into per-contender scores and resolves the overall winnerIndex, treating a tied top score as no winner.

nodes.name: numberRound
nodes.summary: Test helper that builds a numeric Round-shaped fixture with overridable defaults for video-json-builder tests.

nodes.name: buildRound
nodes.summary: Converts a RoundCandidate into the VideoInput Round shape — computing the round winner, encoding values, and picking an SFX cue.

nodes.name: buildVideoInput
nodes.summary: Pure builder that assembles meta, music, contenders, rounds, and the computed verdict into a VideoInput object and validates it against videoInputSchema before returning it.

nodes.name: Root.tsx
nodes.summary: Remotion root component that registers all renderable outputs — the 16:9 and 9:16 comparison video compositions and the two branded thumbnail stills — wiring each to the shared Zod video schema and dynamic duration/resolution calculation.

nodes.name: Crown.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated crown component that drops in with a spring, rotates into place, and gains a colored glow — used to visually mark the winning contender.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines spring() for the drop-in motion with two interpolate() calls (rotation, glow) driven off a delay-adjusted local frame, matching the project's convention of spring for entrances and interpolate for value animation.

nodes.name: Icon.tsx
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper around lucide-react that resolves a kebab-case icon name (e.g. "dollar-sign") to the matching PascalCase Lucide icon component, falling back to HelpCircle when no match exists.

nodes.name: PricePill.tsx
nodes.summary: Presentational pill-shaped badge that displays a contender's price with a dollar icon, themed via the shared Theme object and accented with the contender's color.

nodes.name: ComparisonShort9x16.tsx
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export that aliases Comparison16x9 as ComparisonShort9x16, reusing the exact same scene component for the 9:16 Shorts variant since layout adapts internally from the `meta.aspect` prop.
nodes.languageNotes: Zero-logic re-export — the only difference between the 16:9 and 9:16 outputs lives in the <Composition> registration (id, resolution, example props) in Root.tsx, not in the component itself.

nodes.name: Thumbnail16x9.tsx
nodes.summary: Branded 16:9 still-image thumbnail template (per PROJECT_PLAN.md) rendering both contenders side-by-side with a crown on the winner, price pills, and the video title, generated from the same VideoInput data as the video itself.

nodes.name: Thumbnail16x9B.tsx
nodes.summary: Alternate 16:9 thumbnail template used for A/B testing, using diagonal color-blocked halves per contender instead of the dark panel style of Thumbnail16x9, driven by the identical VideoInput data.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS clip-path polygons to create the diagonal split, a distinct visual technique from the boxed-panel layout in Thumbnail16x9.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Remotion bundler entry point that registers RemotionRoot via registerRoot, bootstrapping the studio app.

nodes.name: RemotionRoot
nodes.summary: Declares the Remotion <Composition>/<Still> registry: Comparison16x9, ComparisonShort9x16, Thumbnail16x9, and Thumbnail16x9B, each bound to the videoInputSchema and given dynamically computed duration/resolution via calculateMetadata.

nodes.name: Crown
nodes.summary: Renders a colored crown icon with a spring drop-in, rotation settle, and glow fade-in, offset by an optional delayFrames prop.

nodes.name: Icon
nodes.summary: React component that looks up and renders a Lucide icon by kebab-case name, forwarding any remaining props (size, color, strokeWidth) to the underlying icon.

nodes.name: PricePill
nodes.summary: Renders a rounded, accent-bordered price badge with a dollar-sign icon, styled from the active theme's surface color, body font, and text color.

nodes.name: ContenderPanel
nodes.summary: Renders one contender's column in the thumbnail — an optional crown slot, the product image in an accent-colored rounded card, and a PricePill — keeping both columns visually aligned regardless of who wins.

nodes.name: Thumbnail16x9
nodes.summary: Top-level thumbnail still composition: parses and validates VideoInput, resolves the theme, then lays out the title, both ContenderPanels with a VS divider, and a "WHO WINS?" call-to-action over the themed Background.

nodes.name: HalfPanel
nodes.summary: Renders one diagonal half of the B-variant thumbnail — a bleeding contender product image plus a name pill (with crown if winner) anchored to the left or right side.

nodes.name: Thumbnail16x9B
nodes.summary: Top-level B-variant thumbnail still composition: parses VideoInput, resolves the theme, and composes two diagonal clip-path halves per contender with a centered VS mark and title banner, for A/B testing against Thumbnail16x9.

nodes.name: AnimatedBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a horizontal bar-race visualization for a spec round, animating each contender's bar width from zero to its normalized value using an eased interpolation.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Remotion's interpolate() with Easing.out(Easing.cubic) per the project's value-animation convention rather than spring().

nodes.name: AnimatedBar
nodes.summary: React component that draws animated comparison bars for each contender, sizing bar width relative to the maximum value and easing the fill-in over the configured reveal window.

nodes.name: Badge.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a badge-style visualization for non-numeric ('badge') spec rounds, springing each contender's badge into view.

nodes.name: Badge
nodes.summary: React component that displays a non-numeric badge value per contender with a spring-based entrance animation, used for qualitative spec rounds.

nodes.name: Counter.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders an animated numeric counter visualization that ticks each contender's value up from zero, preserving unit suffixes and decimal formatting from the original display string.
nodes.languageNotes: Includes small parsing helpers (suffixOf, decimalsOf) that extract non-numeric suffixes and decimal precision from a formatted display value so the animated number keeps the same formatting as the source data.

nodes.name: suffixOf
nodes.summary: Extracts the trailing non-numeric unit suffix (e.g. 'mph', 'GB') from a formatted display value string.

nodes.name: decimalsOf
nodes.summary: Determines the number of decimal places used in a formatted display value so the animated counter matches source precision.

nodes.name: Counter
nodes.summary: React component that animates each contender's numeric spec value counting up over the reveal window, reformatting the interpolated number with the original suffix and decimal precision.

nodes.name: Gauge.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a gauge/dial-style visualization for a spec round, animating a needle or arc fill toward each contender's normalized value.
nodes.languageNotes: Mirrors AnimatedBar's easing and normalization logic (Math.max/Math.abs against the contender set) but renders as a radial gauge instead of a linear bar.

nodes.name: Gauge
nodes.summary: React component that animates a gauge-style dial for each contender, scaling fill/needle position relative to the maximum value in the round with eased interpolation.

nodes.name: LogoSting.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the brand logo sting animation (fade/scale/reveal sequence) used at the start of the Intro scene, themed by the active category theme.

nodes.name: LogoSting
nodes.summary: React component that animates the logo's entrance via chained interpolate() calls for opacity/scale/position, styled using the active theme.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the RoundContenderVisual interface, the shared per-contender shape (name, display value, numeric value, accent color, winner flag) consumed by all round visualization components.

nodes.name: SpecBattle.tsx
nodes.summary: Core comparison scene that sequences each spec round as a Sequence, picking the right visualization component (bar/gauge/counter/badge) per round and rendering it with themed header and icon; exposes getDuration and getSfxCues per the scene contract.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a component lookup map (VISUALIZATION_COMPONENTS) keyed by round.visualization to dynamically select AnimatedBar/Gauge/Counter/Badge, keeping the scene category-agnostic per the project's core architectural rule.

nodes.name: getDuration
nodes.summary: Computes the total duration in frames for the SpecBattle scene based on the number of rounds and the video's fps, per the scene duration contract.

nodes.name: getSfxCues
nodes.summary: Computes sound-effect cue frames for each round's impact moment, resolving each round's sfx key to an audio source via sfxSrc.

nodes.name: RoundCard
nodes.summary: Renders a single round's header (icon + label with spring entrance) and delegates to the round's selected visualization component with per-contender visual data.

nodes.name: SpecBattle
nodes.summary: Top-level scene component that renders the themed background and lays out each round as a timed Sequence of RoundCard instances.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Theme interface and the four built-in category themes (speed, circuit, grid, default) with background gradients, text colors, fonts, and motif, plus getTheme() to resolve a theme key with a safe default fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Centralizes all color/typography values so scenes never hardcode hex values, per the project's theming convention.

nodes.name: getTheme
nodes.summary: Looks up a Theme by key from the themes registry, falling back to the default theme when the key is unrecognized.

nodes.name: batch.ts
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for `pnpm batch --category <slug> --pairs top-<N>` — resolves top-priced VERIFIED products in a category, pairs them into comparisons, builds/saves each comparison, queues a BullMQ render job, and optionally stages staggered publish scheduling and narration synthesis.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses node:util parseArgs for flag parsing and a main().catch().finally() chain to set process.exitCode and disconnect Prisma cleanly on both success and failure.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Parses CLI args, validates the category and pairs/schedule specs, fetches the top-priced VERIFIED products for the category, pairs them, then for each pair builds and saves a comparison, creates a RenderJob, enqueues it on the render queue, and optionally creates a scheduled Upload record.

nodes.name: pair-products.ts
nodes.summary: Utility that takes the top N ranked products and pairs them consecutively into head-to-head matchups for batch comparison generation, dropping any trailing unpaired item.

nodes.name: pairTopRanked
nodes.summary: Slices the top `count` entries from a ranked list and groups them into consecutive [A, B] pairs, dropping an odd leftover item so every video gets exactly two contenders.

nodes.name: pair-products.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering pairTopRanked's consecutive pairing, count capping, odd-item dropping, and empty/short-list edge cases.

nodes.name: parse-pairs-spec.ts
nodes.summary: Parses the `--pairs` CLI flag (e.g. "top-20") into a structured PairsSpec, validating the strategy syntax and enforcing a minimum count of 2.

nodes.name: parsePairsSpec
nodes.summary: Validates and parses a `top-<N>` pairs-strategy string into a `{ strategy, count }` spec, throwing a descriptive error for invalid syntax or counts below 2.

nodes.name: parse-pairs-spec.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests verifying parsePairsSpec accepts valid top-N strategies and rejects too-small counts, unknown strategies, and malformed input.

nodes.name: parse-schedule.ts
nodes.summary: Parses the `--schedule` CLI flag into a stagger interval in milliseconds (or null for immediate publish) and computes each batch item's scheduled publish timestamp.

nodes.name: parseScheduleIntervalMs
nodes.summary: Maps a `--schedule` value (now/hourly/daily/weekly) to a millisecond stagger interval, or null for immediate scheduling, throwing on unrecognized cadences.

nodes.name: scheduledAtFor
nodes.summary: Computes the scheduled publish Date for the i-th batch item by adding `(index + 1) * intervalMs` to the current time.

nodes.name: parse-schedule.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for parseScheduleIntervalMs's cadence parsing and scheduledAtFor's staggered timestamp computation.

nodes.name: render-queue-client.ts
nodes.summary: Producer-side BullMQ queue client that lazily creates a singleton Redis connection and render queue, used by the `pnpm batch` CLI to enqueue render jobs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses module-level lazy singletons (`??=`) to avoid reconnecting to Redis or recreating the Queue instance on repeated calls.

nodes.name: getRenderQueue
nodes.summary: Returns a lazily-initialized singleton BullMQ Queue bound to the render queue, creating the underlying Redis connection on first call.

nodes.name: ingest.ts
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for the `pnpm ingest` command, dispatching to vPIC-based vehicle ingestion or CSV bulk import and upserting the resulting normalized products into the database.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Node's built-in `node:util` parseArgs for lightweight CLI argument parsing instead of a third-party library.

nodes.name: runVpic
nodes.summary: Parses `--make`, `--years`, and `--category` CLI flags, fetches vehicle data via the vPIC ingestion adapter, and upserts each resulting product.

nodes.name: runCsv
nodes.summary: Reads a CSV file path from CLI positionals, resolves it relative to the invoking shell's cwd, parses it into normalized products, and upserts each one.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI dispatcher that reads the subcommand (`vpic` or `csv`) from argv and routes to the matching handler.

nodes.name: parse-years.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests covering parseYears' range expansion, comma-separated list parsing, and error cases for backwards ranges and invalid input.

nodes.name: parse-years.ts
nodes.summary: Small CLI helper that parses the `--years` flag, accepting either an inclusive range ("2024-2026") or an explicit comma-separated list of years.

nodes.name: parseYears
nodes.summary: Converts a year-range or comma-separated year string from CLI input into an array of integer years, throwing on malformed or backwards ranges.

nodes.name: coerce-spec-value.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests verifying coerceSpecValue correctly parses numbers, booleans, and text, and returns null for blank or unparseable input.

nodes.name: coerce-spec-value.ts
nodes.summary: Coerces a raw string spec value (sourced from CSV or vPIC) into the typed number/text/boolean shape expected by a SpecDefinition's declared dataType.

nodes.name: coerceSpecValue
nodes.summary: Given a SpecDataType and a raw string, returns a TypedSpecValue with the appropriate numberValue/textValue/boolValue populated, or null if the value can't be coerced.

nodes.name: upsert-product.ts
nodes.summary: The single point where packages/ingestion's source-agnostic NormalizedProduct output touches Prisma: idempotently upserts category, brand, product, and SpecValue rows keyed on natural keys so re-running an ingest is always safe.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the project's idempotent-jobs convention (CLAUDE.md) by keying every upsert on natural keys (slug, productId+specDefId) rather than surrogate IDs.

nodes.name: upsertNormalizedProduct
nodes.summary: Upserts a NormalizedProduct's category, brand, and product rows, then writes SpecValue rows for both raw string specs (coerced against existing SpecDefinitions) and pre-typed AI-normalized specs.

nodes.name: actions.ts
nodes.summary: Next.js Server Actions for the comparison detail page: idempotently queues a Remotion render job for a comparison's frozen videoJson, and lets a human pick the winning A/B thumbnail variant.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a composite dedupe key (comparisonId + composition + templateVer + non-terminal status) before enqueueing, matching CLAUDE.md's 'idempotent jobs' rule for BullMQ workers.

nodes.name: page.tsx
nodes.summary: Dynamic Next.js route (`/comparisons/[id]`) that loads a comparison with its contenders, latest render job, and latest upload, then renders the video preview, render status, and thumbnail A/B controls.

nodes.name: PreviewPlayer.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component that embeds the Remotion `<Player>` in the dashboard so a comparison's frozen VideoInput can be previewed in-browser before or after rendering.

nodes.name: RenderStatus.tsx
nodes.summary: Client component that polls the render-job status API every few seconds while a render is queued or running, showing progress, errors, and generated thumbnail variants.

nodes.name: queue.ts
nodes.summary: Lazily initializes a process-wide singleton BullMQ Queue and Redis connection used by the dashboard to enqueue render jobs, caching both on globalThis to survive Next.js dev hot-reload.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the `globalThis` caching pattern common in Next.js apps to avoid creating a new Redis connection/Queue instance on every hot-reload in development.

nodes.name: queueRender
nodes.summary: Server action that verifies a comparison has a frozen videoJson, returns an existing non-terminal render job if one matches the current template version (idempotency), or else creates a RenderJob row and enqueues it on the BullMQ render queue.

nodes.name: setThumbnailVariant
nodes.summary: Server action supporting A/B thumbnail testing: persists which generated thumbnail variant an upload should use and revalidates the comparison page.

nodes.name: ComparisonDetailPage
nodes.summary: Async server component for the comparison detail route; fetches the comparison with contenders/renderJobs/uploads, parses its frozen videoJson, and renders the preview player, render-status panel, and queue/A-B-test forms.

nodes.name: PreviewPlayer
nodes.summary: Renders the Remotion `<Player>` bound to the `Comparison16x9` composition, sizing and durating it from the given VideoInput's meta fields.

nodes.name: RenderStatus
nodes.summary: Client component that seeds state from an initial render-job snapshot, then polls `/api/render-jobs/:id` every 3 seconds while the job is QUEUED or RUNNING, rendering progress, errors, and thumbnail variants.

nodes.name: getRenderQueue
nodes.summary: Returns the process-wide singleton BullMQ Queue for render jobs, creating it on first call using a lazily-established Redis connection.

nodes.name: openai-provider.ts
nodes.summary: Default NarrationProvider implementation that calls OpenAI's audio/speech REST endpoint to synthesize TTS narration audio from text, requiring only an API key and no extra client SDK.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a plain fetch() call with response.ok/status checks and Buffer.from(await response.arrayBuffer()) rather than a vendor SDK, keeping the provider dependency-free.

nodes.name: provider.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the NarrationProvider interface — the single contract TTS providers must implement to plug into synthesizeNarration(), keeping provider swaps isolated from script-building and studio audio playback.

nodes.name: script.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest suite for buildNarrationScript that verifies it emits one line per hook/round/verdict, mentions both contenders and names the round winner, keeps timeline anchors strictly increasing, and preserves the verdict tagline verbatim.

nodes.name: script.ts
nodes.summary: Turns a frozen, category-agnostic VideoInput into timestamped narration lines (hook, one per round, verdict) by anchoring each line to scene and round timeline boundaries computed by the shared package.
nodes.languageNotes: Accepts only Pick<VideoInput, 'meta'|'contenders'|'rounds'|'verdict'> so the function stays pure and never needs category-specific knowledge (CLAUDE.md prime directive #1).

nodes.name: synthesize.ts
nodes.summary: Orchestrates end-to-end narration synthesis: builds the script, invokes the injected TTS provider per line, writes mp3 files to disk, probes real audio duration via music-metadata, and returns NarrationClip[] ready to attach to a VideoInput.
nodes.languageNotes: Probes real mp3 duration with music-metadata's parseBuffer rather than trusting the provider or a word-count estimate, since AudioTrack's ducking window depends on accurate durationSec.

nodes.name: OpenAiTtsProvider
nodes.summary: Concrete NarrationProvider backed by OpenAI's /v1/audio/speech endpoint; reads the API key from options or OPENAI_API_KEY, posts text with configurable model/voice, and returns the resulting mp3 as a Buffer.

nodes.name: buildNarrationScript
nodes.summary: Computes narration lines with absolute timeline anchors (hook, per-round, verdict) from a VideoInput's meta/contenders/rounds/verdict, using shared scene- and round-time-range helpers so it stays category-agnostic.

nodes.name: synthesizeNarration
nodes.summary: Async pipeline that scripts, synthesizes via an injected NarrationProvider, saves one mp3 per narration line, probes real audio duration, and returns NarrationClip[] for attaching to a VideoInput.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Prisma client singleton for the db package, memoized on the global object outside production to survive dev hot-reload without exhausting database connections, and re-exports all generated Prisma types and enums for use across the monorepo.
nodes.languageNotes: Classic Node/Prisma singleton pattern: stash the client on globalThis in dev so hot-reload doesn't spawn a new PrismaClient (and new connection pool) on every file change.

nodes.name: products.ts
nodes.summary: Hand-written demo catalog data providing extra cars plus a full phone and laptop roster (with per-spec numeric/text/bool values), satisfying CLAUDE.md's requirement to seed 4 cars, 4 phones, and 2 laptops with complete specs out of the box.

nodes.name: spec-definitions.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the full SpecDefinition catalogs (label, unit, data type, visualization, priority weight, sort order) for the cars, phones, and laptops demo categories, exposed per-category via SPEC_DEFINITIONS_BY_CATEGORY.
nodes.languageNotes: The car SpecDefinition set is deliberately kept in sync with the rounds in examples/comparison-example.json so the video payload seed and this catalog seed upsert the same rows rather than diverging.

nodes.name: seed.ts
nodes.summary: Database seed script run via `pnpm db:seed`: loads examples/comparison-example.json to upsert the cars category, its SpecDefinitions, Products, and Comparison, then seeds the additional cars, phones, and laptops demo catalogs — all as idempotent upserts keyed by natural keys per CLAUDE.md's idempotent-jobs directive.

nodes.name: upsertSpecDefinitions
nodes.summary: Upserts a category's list of SpecDefinition seed rows and returns a Map from spec key to the persisted SpecDefinition record so callers can look up spec def ids by key.

nodes.name: seedDemoProduct
nodes.summary: Upserts one hand-authored demo Product, its Brand, and each of its SpecValue rows for a given category; deliberately skips creating a ProductImage since these catalog rows have no licensed image asset yet.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point of the seed script: loads the example video payload, upserts the cars category/SpecDefinitions/Products/Comparison from it, then seeds the phones and laptops categories from the demo catalogs, logging a summary of what was seeded.

nodes.name: vpic.test.ts
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the vPIC vehicle adapter, covering model fetching against a JSON fixture, mapping raw results into DRAFT NormalizedProduct records, and the rate-limited/deduped multi-year ingest flow.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a fixture file (vpic-models-toyota-2026.json) and a vi.fn-based fake fetch to avoid real network calls, asserting on elapsed time to verify rate-limiting.

nodes.name: vpic.ts
nodes.summary: NHTSA vPIC adapter that fetches make/model vehicle data for a given year and maps it into DRAFT NormalizedProduct records; enrichment (price, specs) happens later via CSV import or AI normalizer per PROJECT_PLAN.md.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-imposes a rate limit via createRateLimiter even though vPIC publishes no rate limit, to stay a polite consumer across large batch ingests.

nodes.name: http.ts
nodes.summary: Shared HTTP utility module providing createRateLimiter, a minimal throttling helper, and the FetchLike type alias used to inject fetch implementations across ingestion adapters.

nodes.name: fetchModelsForMakeYear
nodes.summary: Calls the vPIC GetModelsForMakeYear endpoint for a given make and year, throwing an error on a non-ok response and returning the parsed Results array.

nodes.name: mapVpicResultsToProducts
nodes.summary: Transforms raw vPIC model results into DRAFT NormalizedProduct objects, title-casing the brand and stashing the raw vPIC make/model IDs into the specs blob for traceability.

nodes.name: ingestVpicMakeYears
nodes.summary: Orchestrates a rate-limited vPIC ingest across multiple model years for one make, deduplicating products by (brand, model, year) before returning the combined list; safe to re-run since downstream upserts key on the product slug.

nodes.name: createRateLimiter
nodes.summary: Factory that produces a throttle() helper enforcing a minimum interval between the start of consecutive async calls, used by ingestion adapters to avoid hammering external sources.

nodes.name: video-input.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the sacred Zod-validated VideoInput schema (meta, music, contenders, rounds, verdict, optional narration) that is the single contract the comparison builder produces and the Remotion studio composition consumes — per CLAUDE.md prime directive #1, no category-specific logic may leak past this shape. Exposes parseVideoInput() to validate a raw payload and throw a readable, path-annotated error on failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Zod's .refine() for cross-field invariants (round.values/displayValues parallel-array lengths, and per-contender array lengths matching contenders.length) rather than encoding them in separate TypeScript types — validation and type inference (z.infer) stay in lockstep from one schema definition.

nodes.name: parseVideoInput
nodes.summary: Safely parses an unknown payload against videoInputSchema, throwing a formatted multi-line error listing every Zod validation issue (field path + message) when the payload is invalid, otherwise returning the typed VideoInput.

nodes.name: timing.ts
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for scene durations (TIMING_SECONDS) authored in seconds per the Remotion convention in CLAUDE.md, plus pure helpers that compute absolute scene start times, per-round time ranges, and total composition duration from a VideoInput — reused by the Remotion renderer, the analytics feedback loop, and narration timing without duplicating the arithmetic.
nodes.languageNotes: Keeps all timing math in seconds (not frames) so it stays fps-independent; conversion to frames only happens via the separate secondsToFrames() helper at the point of use inside Remotion, matching the CLAUDE.md rule that frame counts must never be hardcoded.

nodes.name: secondsToFrames
nodes.summary: Converts a duration in seconds to a rounded frame count for a given fps, the sole place frame-count rounding happens.

nodes.name: contenderRevealSeconds
nodes.summary: Computes the ContenderReveal scene's duration in seconds as a base plus a per-contender increment, so the scene scales with however many contenders are in the video.

nodes.name: specBattleSeconds
nodes.summary: Computes the total SpecBattle scene duration as a fixed per-round duration multiplied by the number of rounds.

nodes.name: computeSceneBoundariesSec
nodes.summary: Computes the absolute start second of every scene (Intro, ContenderReveal, SpecBattle, WinnerReveal, Outro) on the full composition timeline, overlapping each pair by one sceneTransition — mirrors Comparison16x9.tsx's own scene-start computation and acts as the single source of truth other timing helpers build on.

nodes.name: computeTotalDurationSec
nodes.summary: Returns the total composition duration in seconds by adding the outro's own duration to its computed start second.

nodes.name: computeRoundTimeRangesSec
nodes.summary: Produces an absolute [startSec, endSec) time range for every SpecBattle round within the full composition timeline, used to correlate narration and retention analytics back to individual rounds.

nodes.name: timing.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for packages/shared/src/timing.ts, verifying scene boundary overlap-by-one-transition behavior, per-round time range layout, and that total duration grows correctly with more rounds.

nodes.name: 20260720081004_init/migration.sql
nodes.summary: Initial Prisma migration establishing the core Versus Engine schema: Category, Brand, Product, ProductImage, SpecDefinition, SpecValue, Comparison, ComparisonContender, RenderJob, and Upload tables plus their unique and lookup indexes.

nodes.name: Category
nodes.summary: Top-level product category (cars, phones, laptops, ...) keyed by slug, carrying a themeKey used to select the category's Remotion visual theme.

nodes.name: Brand
nodes.summary: Manufacturer/brand reference table (name, slug, logo, country) referenced by Product rows.

nodes.name: Product
nodes.summary: Core product entity (e.g. a specific car/phone/laptop model) storing raw specs as JSONB, provenance fields (source, sourceUrl, verifiedAt), status, and accentColor for video theming; only VERIFIED rows are eligible for rendering.

nodes.name: ProductImage
nodes.summary: Product image assets (hero shots, thumbnails, etc.) with license/attribution metadata and dimensions, linked to a Product.

nodes.name: SpecDefinition
nodes.summary: Category-scoped definition of a comparable spec (key, unit, dataType, visualization, priorityWeight) that drives the round-selection heuristic and how a spec is rendered as a video scene.

nodes.name: SpecValue
nodes.summary: Normalized, SI-unit comparable value for one Product/SpecDefinition pair, storing numeric/text/bool forms plus a display string and confidence score for AI-assisted extraction.

nodes.name: Comparison
nodes.summary: A generated comparison video record: title/tagline/tags, the frozen videoJson payload used to re-render identically, computed winnerIndex/scores, and publish status.

nodes.name: ComparisonContender
nodes.summary: Join table linking a Comparison to its contending Products in a fixed left/right position order.

nodes.name: RenderJob
nodes.summary: Tracks a BullMQ render job for a Comparison (composition, template version, progress, output/thumbnail URLs, error), designed to be idempotently keyed by comparisonId + templateVer.

nodes.name: Upload
nodes.summary: Tracks publishing a rendered Comparison to an external platform (e.g. YouTube), recording platform videoId, scheduling, and publish status/errors.

nodes.name: 20260720190000_phase_5_ab_thumbnails_and_analytics/migration.sql
nodes.summary: Phase 5 migration adding YouTube analytics tracking: VideoAnalytics (view/impression/retention metrics per upload) and RoundRetention (per-round audience retention and drop-off tied to comparisons and spec categories) tables.

nodes.name: VideoAnalytics
nodes.summary: Aggregate YouTube performance metrics (views, impressions, CTR, average view duration/percentage, retention curve) fetched for a single Upload.

nodes.name: RoundRetention
nodes.summary: Per-round audience retention breakdown (retention at start/end, drop-off) for a specific comparison round/spec, used to feed the round-selection heuristic with real viewer-retention data.

nodes.name: migration_lock.toml
nodes.summary: Prisma migration lock file pinning the database provider to PostgreSQL so all generated migrations stay consistent across environments.

nodes.name: schema.prisma
nodes.summary: Canonical, category-agnostic data model for Versus Engine: Category/Brand/Product catalog, a scalable SpecDefinition/SpecValue system for arbitrary comparable attributes, Comparison/ComparisonContender/RenderJob/Upload for the video pipeline, and a Phase 5 VideoAnalytics/RoundRetention feedback loop. This is the single source of truth referenced throughout CLAUDE.md.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Prisma enums (ProductStatus, SpecDataType, SpecVisualization, ComparisonStatus, JobStatus) to keep category logic entirely data-driven — new product categories are added via SpecDefinition rows rather than schema or code changes, per the project's prime directive against category-specific branching.

nodes.name: Category
nodes.summary: Top-level product category (cars, phones, laptops, motorcycles) that scopes products, spec definitions, and comparisons, and maps to a studio theme.

nodes.name: Brand
nodes.summary: Manufacturer/brand record (name, logo, country) associated with one or more products.

nodes.name: Product
nodes.summary: Core catalog entity for a single product (e.g. a car trim or phone model), storing raw untouched specs as JSON, verification status, source provenance, and the extracted accent color used for theming.

nodes.name: ProductImage
nodes.summary: Processed product image asset (hero, side, cutout, etc.) with licensing/attribution metadata, produced by the ingestion image pipeline.

nodes.name: SpecDefinition
nodes.summary: Per-category definition of a comparable attribute (label, unit, data type, visualization style, priority weight) — the scalability core that lets new product categories be added purely via data rows.

nodes.name: SpecValue
nodes.summary: Normalized, comparable value of a SpecDefinition for a given product, storing the SI/canonical number alongside the human-readable display string and an AI-confidence score.

nodes.name: Comparison
nodes.summary: A generated video comparison between contenders, holding the frozen videoJson payload actually rendered, YouTube metadata, and per-contender round scores for reproducible re-rendering.

nodes.name: ComparisonContender
nodes.summary: Join table linking a Comparison to the Products competing in it, ordered by position (0 = left/first).

nodes.name: RenderJob
nodes.summary: Tracks a single Remotion render invocation for a Comparison — composition variant, template version, progress, output URL, and A/B thumbnail variants.

nodes.name: Upload
nodes.summary: Tracks publishing a rendered comparison to a platform (YouTube), including the selected A/B thumbnail variant and publish scheduling.

nodes.name: VideoAnalytics
nodes.summary: Phase 5 feedback-loop entity capturing YouTube performance metrics (views, CTR, retention curve) for an uploaded video.

nodes.name: RoundRetention
nodes.summary: Per-round audience retention breakdown (start/end/drop-off) linked back to a SpecDefinition key, letting the round-selection heuristic learn which spec rounds retain viewers across many comparisons.

nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests for slugify and keyify, covering lowercasing, dash trimming, and special-character (degree sign, en/em dash) handling.

nodes.name: slug.ts
nodes.summary: String helpers for generating URL-safe product slugs (slugify) and SpecDefinition.key identifiers (keyify) used across ingestion and the dashboard.

nodes.name: slugify
nodes.summary: Converts a display name (e.g. "Toyota GR Corolla") into a URL-safe lowercase, dash-separated identifier.

nodes.name: keyify
nodes.summary: Converts a human-readable spec label (e.g. "0-100 km/h") into a snake_case SpecDefinition.key, normalizing degree signs and dashes.

nodes.name: specs.ts
nodes.summary: Zod schemas defining the common output contract for every ingestion adapter (vPIC, CSV, AI normalizer, scrapers): normalized spec values, normalized products, and category SpecDefinition seed data, all consumed by packages/db upserts.

nodes.name: units.test.ts
nodes.summary: Vitest unit tests verifying imperial-to-SI unit conversion accuracy for weight (lb to kg), speed (mph to km/h), and torque (ft-lb to Nm).

nodes.name: units.ts
nodes.summary: Unit conversion helpers (weight, speed, length, torque, temperature) plus a rounding utility, used to normalize raw imperial specs into canonical SI-ish values once at ingestion time per project convention.
nodes.languageNotes: Each conversion is a pure one-line arithmetic function, keeping the ingestion-time normalization step easy to audit and test in isolation.

nodes.name: lbToKg
nodes.summary: Converts pounds to kilograms using the exact avoirdupois pound conversion factor.

nodes.name: mphToKmh
nodes.summary: Converts miles per hour to kilometers per hour.

nodes.name: inToCm
nodes.summary: Converts inches to centimeters.

nodes.name: inToMm
nodes.summary: Converts inches to millimeters.

nodes.name: ozToG
nodes.summary: Converts ounces to grams.

nodes.name: ftLbToNm
nodes.summary: Converts foot-pounds of torque to newton-meters.

nodes.name: fToC
nodes.summary: Converts degrees Fahrenheit to degrees Celsius.

nodes.name: round
nodes.summary: Rounds a number to a given decimal precision (default 1), used to tidy converted spec values before display or comparison.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript project configuration for packages/shared, extending the monorepo's base tsconfig, compiling src to dist with no emitted output during type-checking-only builds.

layers.name: Video Rendering Engine
layers.description: The Remotion composition (apps/studio) that consumes the Zod-validated VideoInput JSON contract and renders comparison videos through scenes, components, audio ducking, and theming, with zero category-specific logic.

layers.name: Admin Dashboard
layers.description: The Next.js 15 App Router application (apps/dashboard) for browsing products and comparisons, previewing renders, and queuing new video jobs.

layers.name: Workers & CLI
layers.description: BullMQ job workers and operator CLI scripts (apps/workers) that ingest data, trigger renders, publish videos, sync analytics, and deploy Lambda/YouTube integrations.

layers.name: Comparison Engine
layers.description: The round-selection and scoring heuristic (packages/comparison) that turns verified product specs into the frozen VideoInput JSON payload for rendering.

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: The Prisma schema, SQL migrations, and database client/seed data (database/ and packages/db) defining and accessing the 12-table Postgres model for products, specs, and comparisons.

layers.name: Ingestion Pipeline
layers.description: Source adapters (vPIC API, CSV), image processing, and AI-assisted normalization (packages/ingestion) that pull raw specs into the Product/SpecValue data model.

layers.name: Narration Layer
layers.description: The optional text-to-speech layer (packages/narration) that generates and synthesizes narration scripts for comparison videos.

layers.name: Shared Utilities & Contracts
layers.description: Cross-cutting Zod schemas, timing constants, unit conversion, slug/CSV helpers, and BullMQ queue-name constants (packages/shared) consumed by every app and package.

layers.name: Vendored Agent-Skill Docs
layers.description: Bundled third-party Claude Code agent-skill documentation trees (.agents/skills, agent/skills) for Remotion and Mediabunny tooling, not part of the Versus Engine application itself.

layers.name: Project Governance & Build Config
layers.description: Root-level monorepo configuration, workspace/build tooling, the local Docker dev stack (Postgres/Redis/MinIO), and top-level docs and example payloads that govern the project as a whole.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what Versus Engine is: a category-agnostic engine that turns Postgres records into animated "X vs Y" comparison videos for any product category using Remotion. It introduces the architectural idea that recurs throughout the codebase — a strict separation between category-agnostic data (Category -> Brand -> Product -> SpecValue) and a renderer that only knows about a Zod-validated video contract, never about "cars" or "phones" specifically. Keep this data/rendering split in mind; nearly every subsequent stop on this tour exists either to produce that contract or to consume it.

tour.title: Architecture Blueprint & Prime Directives
tour.description: PROJECT_PLAN.md is the project's source of truth for architecture and the six-phase build roadmap, detailing the data layer design and the Zod VideoInput contract at a level of detail the README only summarizes. CLAUDE.md translates that plan into enforceable engineering rules: the video JSON contract is "sacred" and must never leak category-specific logic, Phase 1 (a single polished rendered MP4) comes before any dashboard or scraper work, rendered payloads are frozen for reproducible re-renders, and all BullMQ jobs must be idempotent. These two documents govern every design decision you'll see in the rest of the tour, especially the boundary between the comparison engine and the renderer.

tour.title: The Category-Agnostic Data Model
tour.description: database/schema.prisma is the canonical Postgres schema implementing the Category -> Brand -> Product -> SpecValue design from Step 2. The key scalability trick is SpecDefinition: instead of hardcoding columns like "horsepower" or "screen size", each category declares its own set of comparable attributes (label, unit, dataType, visualization, priorityWeight) as data rows, so adding a new category means inserting SpecDefinition rows, not writing code. Product stores raw scraped specs untouched in JSONB while SpecValue holds the normalized, unit-converted numbers the comparison engine actually compares — a raw/normalized split you'll see enforced again later in the ingestion pipeline.
tour.languageLesson: Prisma schema files declare models that map 1:1 to SQL tables; relations (like Product.specValues) become foreign keys and are turned into type-safe Prisma Client accessors in packages/db, so the ORM layer and the SQL schema never drift apart.

tour.title: The Sacred VideoInput Contract
tour.description: This fixture is the exact JSON payload that `pnpm render` must accept — a Toyota GR Corolla vs Honda Civic Type R comparison with meta, looping music, two contenders, scored rounds, and a verdict. It's the tangible artifact of CLAUDE.md's Prime Directive #1: whatever shape this file has is the only shape the Remotion renderer is allowed to understand. Study its structure now, because every remaining stop either produces a payload like this (the comparison engine) or consumes one (the studio renderer) — nothing in between ever branches on "is this a car."

tour.title: The Zod Schema Enforcing the Contract
tour.description: packages/shared/src/video-input.ts turns the informal JSON shape from Step 4 into an enforced Zod schema — the actual sacred contract referenced everywhere else in the codebase — with parseVideoInput() throwing a readable, path-annotated error the instant a payload strays from it. packages/shared/src/timing.ts is the single source of truth for scene durations in seconds (never hardcoded frame counts), plus helpers that compute absolute scene start times and per-round time ranges from a VideoInput. Both live in packages/shared specifically so the comparison engine, the Remotion renderer, and the narration/analytics packages can all import the exact same math and validation instead of re-deriving it.
tour.languageLesson: Zod schemas act as both a runtime validator and, via z.infer<typeof Schema>, a compile-time TypeScript type generator — this is how the project gets one source of truth for a shape instead of a hand-written interface and a separate validator that could drift apart.

tour.title: The Remotion Entry Point
tour.description: apps/studio/src/index.ts is the literal entry point Remotion's bundler loads — it just calls registerRoot() with the component from Root.tsx. Root.tsx is where every renderable output is declared: the 16:9 and 9:16 comparison video compositions plus the two branded thumbnail stills, each wired to the shared Zod video schema from Step 5 for dynamic duration/resolution calculation. This is the first place category-agnostic data and rendering formally meet — Root.tsx doesn't know what a comparison is about, only that it needs a valid VideoInput.

tour.title: The Root Composition
tour.description: Comparison16x9.tsx is the composition Phase 1 must render end-to-end per CLAUDE.md: it validates the incoming VideoInput, resolves the category theme and portrait/landscape layout, sequences all six scenes with TransitionSeries, overlays the persistent Scoreboard, and mounts the audio track. schema.ts simply re-exports the Zod schema from packages/shared so the renderer validates against the exact same contract the comparison engine produces — never a locally drifted copy. Notice the fan-in here: nearly every scene and component you'll see next imports schema.ts, timing.ts, and theme/index.ts, making this composition the structural hub of the entire studio app.

tour.title: The Six-Scene Video Template
tour.description: These three scenes illustrate the six-scene template (Hook Intro -> Contender Reveal -> Spec Battles -> Scoreboard -> Winner Reveal -> Outro) that Comparison16x9.tsx sequences. Intro.tsx splits the "X VS Y" title from the video's title string with spring-animated entrances; SpecBattle.tsx is the core scene, sequencing each spec round and picking a bar/gauge/counter/badge visualization based on the round's data type; WinnerReveal.tsx reveals the winner with a Crown and a Confetti burst colored by the contender's accent. Each scene exports getDuration() and getSfxCues() per the CLAUDE.md scene contract, so audio placement is computed from the data rather than eyeballed.
tour.languageLesson: Remotion scenes drive all animation from useVideoConfig().fps and useCurrentFrame() rather than wall-clock time — spring() handles entrances and interpolate() with eased curves handles value animations like the bar races in SpecBattle, so the same code produces a deterministic frame at render time regardless of render speed.

tour.title: Theming & Audio Design Patterns
tour.description: theme/index.ts defines the Theme interface and four built-in category themes (speed, circuit, grid, default) with gradients, fonts, and motifs — this is why colors are never hardcoded in any scene component you saw in Step 8; they always come from getTheme() plus the contender's own accentColor. music.ts is the pure audio-math module building the per-frame music volume envelope described in CLAUDE.md: a base level, -6dB ducking around SFX/narration cues, and a 2-second fade-out, all computed rather than manually keyframed. Together these two files show the project's general pattern of pushing "what to draw" into data-driven configuration and "how it changes over time" into small, independently testable pure functions.

tour.title: The Comparison Engine
tour.description: This is where the category-agnostic data from Step 3 becomes a VideoInput payload. round-selection.ts implements the CLAUDE.md heuristic verbatim: score each SpecDefinition candidate by priorityWeight x normalizedDifference, take the top 6-8, always include price, cap badge rounds at one, and guarantee each contender wins at least one round. video-json-builder.ts is the pure function that turns those selected rounds into the Zod-validated shape from Step 5, and build-comparison.ts is the Prisma-backed orchestrator that ties it together — loading VERIFIED products, running selection and scoring, generating AI metadata (with a deterministic fallback), and idempotently freezing the result by slug so it can be re-rendered identically later.

tour.title: Getting Data Into the Model
tour.description: vpic.ts shows one concrete source adapter: it fetches vehicle data from the NHTSA vPIC API and maps it into DRAFT NormalizedProduct records — always DRAFT until a human approves it in the dashboard, per CLAUDE.md's AI-content rule. upsert-product.ts is the single choke point where that source-agnostic output actually touches Prisma, idempotently upserting Category/Brand/Product/SpecValue rows keyed on natural keys, so re-running the same ingest twice is always safe. seed.ts uses the same idempotent-upsert pattern to load the demo catalog (4 cars, 4 phones, 2 laptops) from comparison-example.json and hand-written seed data, which is how the project guarantees demos work out of the box.

tour.title: BullMQ Workers & Batch CLI
tour.description: render-worker.ts is the consumer that actually invokes Remotion — either a local bundle or Remotion Lambda depending on RENDER_TARGET — on the frozen videoJson produced by build-comparison.ts in Step 10, then enqueues a follow-up publish job. publish-worker.ts uploads that finished render to YouTube via the Data API, honoring scheduled publish times, and safely no-ops if the upload already has a videoId — a direct application of CLAUDE.md's idempotent-jobs directive. batch.ts is the `pnpm batch` CLI entry point that ties the whole pipeline together operationally: pick top-priced VERIFIED products in a category, pair them up, build/save each comparison, and queue the renders.

tour.title: The Admin Dashboard
tour.description: The Next.js dashboard is where a human closes the loop the automated pipeline can't: page.tsx shows category counts and DRAFT/VERIFIED product stats, new/actions.ts is the Server Action that validates selected products and calls the same build-comparison.ts orchestrator from Step 10 to create a new comparison, and PreviewPlayer.tsx embeds Remotion's own <Player> component so a comparison's frozen VideoInput can be previewed in-browser before committing to a full render. This is the same VideoInput contract from Step 4 the whole way through — the dashboard never has its own notion of what a video looks like, it just previews and triggers the same renderer.

tour.title: Optional AI Narration
tour.description: script.ts turns a frozen VideoInput into timestamped narration lines (one hook, one per round, one verdict), anchoring each line to the exact scene and round timeline boundaries computed by packages/shared/src/timing.ts from Step 5 — so narration timing and visual timing can never drift apart. synthesize.ts orchestrates the rest: it builds the script, calls an injectable TTS provider per line, writes the resulting mp3 files to disk, and probes their real audio duration so the final NarrationClip[] can be attached back onto the VideoInput before it's rendered. This package is a good example of the project's plug-in philosophy — a narrow NarrationProvider interface means swapping TTS vendors never touches script-building or studio playback code.

tour.title: Local Development Infrastructure
tour.description: docker-compose.yml provisions the three stateful services every other step in this tour depends on locally: Postgres 16 for the schema from Step 3, Redis 7 for the BullMQ queues from Step 12, and MinIO as an S3-compatible store for product images and rendered videos. Each service has a health check and a persistent named volume, and the exposed ports/credentials match exactly what .env.example expects — this is the file that makes `pnpm dev` actually work end-to-end on a laptop before anything touches real cloud infrastructure.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose's depends_on plus healthcheck blocks let dependent containers (or dependent pnpm scripts) wait for Postgres/Redis to be truly ready to accept connections, not just for the container process to have started — a common source of flaky local-dev races when skipped.
